content: Existing Self-Refine frameworks for LLMs can be significantly enhanced by integrating multiple inconsistent self-perspectives from modern LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a two-layer self-refinement structure that still retains the original method's key advantage of requiring no extra supervised training or external data. This structure first lets the LLM generate conflicting critical perspectives on its own initial output, then uses these diverse self-feedback signals to drive iterative self-improvement, rather than refining based on a single implicit self-evaluation.
